name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value larger than the original input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0x1_0000_0000_0000usize,
        b < 0x1_0000_0000_0000usize,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize should always be aligned to b; if not, the spec is wrong

name: aligned_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_broken(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
        a <= usize::MAX - b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If a is already aligned to b, align_to_usize should be the identity; deviation indicates inconsistency

name: align_to_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_to_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to 1 should always return the input unchanged since every integer is 1-aligned

name: aligned_zero_size_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_zero_size_vacuous(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
        addr < MAX_PHYADDR,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: aligned with size 0 causes division by zero in modulo; if this verifies, the spec admits undefined behavior
